mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, directly increasing equity valuations. A sustained month-over-month decline in FEDFUNDS produces positive S&P 500 forward returns within 20 trading days as market reprices equity risk premium downward.
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive 20-day forward returns occur in less than 55% of instances where FEDFUNDS declines month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer purchasing power, triggering equity sell-offs as investors anticipate lower corporate earnings. When UNRATE increases by more than 0.25 percentage points within a month, S&P 500 experiences negative 5-day forward returns.
failure_condition: Negative 5-day forward returns occur in less than 50% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.25pp, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 18-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales growth (RSAFS) reflects consumer demand strength and future corporate revenue expectations. Accelerating month-over-month retail sales growth produces positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as earnings revisions flow upward and equity risk premium compresses.
failure_condition: Positive 60-day forward returns occur in less than 52% of instances where RSAFS growth accelerates, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) and payroll employment (PAYEMS) jointly signal real economic capacity utilization. When both INDPRO and PAYEMS grow simultaneously above their 3-month trend, the economy operates at high utilization, supporting equity valuations through earnings growth. This interaction effect produces positive 20-day returns.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns occur in less than 54% of instances where both INDPRO and PAYEMS grow above 3-month trend, or interaction Rank IC < 0.06 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation measured by CPIAUCSL above 3% combined with FEDFUNDS below 3% signals real negative rates, which erodes bond returns and forces capital reallocation to equities. This interaction produces positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as real rate compression drives equity demand.
failure_condition: Positive 60-day forward returns occur in less than 53% of instances where real rates are negative, or interaction Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) decline signals weakening future consumer wealth and construction employment, reducing aggregate demand. When HOUST falls below 1100K units (annualized), S&P 500 experiences negative 20-day forward returns as investors anticipate demand contraction.
failure_condition: Negative 20-day forward returns occur in less than 50% of instances where HOUST < 1100K, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 18-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth decelerates below 100K per month while UNRATE remains stable, signaling labor market slack development. This interaction indicates future wage pressure relief and margin expansion, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS monthly growth < 100K and UNRATE stable within 0.1pp
regime_hint: labor market softening with stable unemployment
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns occur in less than 52% of instances where PAYEMS growth < 100K and UNRATE stable, or interaction Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) measured quarterly decelerates below 2% annualized rate, signaling late-cycle slowdown and reduced earnings growth expectations. When GDPC1 growth falls below 2%, S&P 500 experiences negative 60-day forward returns as terminal growth rate expectations compress.
failure_condition: Negative 60-day forward returns occur in less than 51% of instances where GDPC1 growth < 2%, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.
